channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Menna Fitzpatrick Returns from Injury to Join Great Britain Squad for Winter Paralympics
item.description: Great Britain’s most decorated Winter Paralympian, Menna Fitzpatrick, has been selected to compete at the upcoming Winter Paralympics in Milan-Cortina, despite suffering a significant knee injury in D...

item.title: Savannah Guthrie’s Family Announces $1m Reward in Search for Missing 84-Year-Old Mother
item.description: Savannah Guthrie has announced that her family is offering a $1m reward for information leading to the return of her 84-year-old mother, Nancy Guthrie, who has been missing for 24 days. Speaking throu...

item.title: Iran’s Supreme Leader Khamenei killed in US-Israeli airstrikes along with family members
item.description: Iran has officially confirmed the death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, the Supreme Leader of the country, who was killed in a joint airstrike by US and Israeli forces. The airstrike, which targeted his co...

item.title: University of Southampton Student Union to Make Plant-Based Meals the Default from 2026
item.description: The University of Southampton has announced that plant-based meals will become the default option at its student union catering facilities starting in the 2026/27 academic year, following a vote by it...

item.title: Couple on honeymoon in Dubai recounts missile strike on their hotel
item.description: A couple on their honeymoon in Dubai have shared their terrifying experience after their hotel was struck by a missile. Matthew McGinn and Layla Hamood, from Liverpool, were staying at the Fairmont Pa...

item.title: Europe Expands Orbital Launch Capabilities from Arctic and Northern Spaceports
item.description: The Esrange Space Centre in Swedish Lapland, run by the Swedish Space Corporation (SSC), is emerging as a key site in Europe's expanding orbital launch ambitions. Known for over 600 sub-orbital rocket...

item.title: UK government proposes ban on unlicensed betting firms sponsoring football clubs
item.description: The UK government is considering a ban on sports sponsorship by gambling companies that do not hold a UK licence, aiming to protect consumers and uphold regulatory standards. Ministers have expressed ...

item.title: Actor Robert Carradine dies at 71 after long struggle with bipolar disorder
item.description: Robert Carradine, the American actor widely recognised for his roles in the 1980s comedy franchise 'Revenge of the Nerds' and the Disney Channel series 'Lizzie McGuire', has died at the age of 71. In ...

item.title: Fulu Foundation Offers $10,000 to Hackers Who Can Stop Ring Cameras from Sharing Data with Amazon
item.description: The Fulu Foundation, a nonprofit organization focused on removing user-hostile features from tech devices, is offering a $10,000 bounty for anyone who can hack Ring's video doorbell cameras to prevent...

item.title: Senate and House to Vote on War Powers Resolution to Prevent Unilateral War on Iran by Trump
item.description: Senate Minority Leader Chuck Schumer and House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries initially attempted to quash a bipartisan War Powers Resolution that demands President Trump seek approval from Congress ...

item.title: The Decline in Strawberry Sweetness: What’s Behind It?
item.description: Strawberries have become less sweet in recent years, and it's not just a matter of bad luck at the grocery store. A 2021 study published in *Horticulture Research* found that many people feel store-bo...

item.title: Influencers caught in Dubai as Iranian missile attacks wreak havoc across the city
item.description: Influencers and celebrities in Dubai were caught in a terrifying missile and drone attack launched by Iran, with plumes of smoke rising from the Fairmont hotel on Palm Jumeirah. Among those trapped in...

item.title: UK to Extend Ofcom Broadcasting Rules to Major Streaming Platforms
item.description: The UK government is introducing new legislation that will require major video-on-demand (VoD) platforms to comply with the same content and accessibility standards as traditional television broadcast...

item.title: Russell Brand denies new and previous sexual assault and rape allegations in London court
item.description: Russell Brand, the 50-year-old broadcaster, actor, and comedian, has pleaded not guilty to two additional charges of rape and sexual assault at Southwark Crown Court in London. The alleged incidents a...

item.title: Former British Ambassador Peter Mandelson Arrested Amid Misconduct Investigation
item.description: Peter Mandelson, the former British ambassador to the United States and ex-government minister, has been arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office. Authorities are investigating allegations...

item.title: North Berwick residents protest against new parking meters amidst widespread vandalism
item.description: The picturesque town of North Berwick has become the centre of intense opposition to the introduction of parking meters by East Lothian Council. Residents, many of whom are elderly, have expressed str...

item.title: Coventry Charity Struggles to Stay Open Amid Declining Donations
item.description: Clothing Coventry, a charity that provides free clothing to vulnerable people and families, is facing possible closure unless it secures additional funding. Established in 2020, the organization relie...

item.title: Christina Applegate Discusses the Challenges of Living with MS and Her Journey Towards Honest Self-Reflection
item.description: Christina Applegate, the Emmy-winning star, shares a deeply personal account of her life since being diagnosed with multiple sclerosis (MS) in 2021. In her memoir, Applegate describes how MS has affec...

item.title: Thirty Swans Found Dead in East London as Authorities Investigate Possible Avian Flu
item.description: Health and environmental authorities are investigating the deaths of 30 swans found in the River Thames over the course of a week, raising concerns about a possible new outbreak of avian flu in the Un...

item.title: FBI Investigates Prior Visit by Suspect Before Nancy Guthrie’s Disappearance
item.description: Authorities investigating the disappearance of Nancy Guthrie, mother of NBC News anchor Savannah Guthrie, say that the prime suspect may have been at her home prior to the night she went missing. The ...
